sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body:
### Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables Class 10: Chapter 3

CBSE Class 10 Maths Chapter 3 Pairs of Linear Equations in Two Variables notes are available here. This article is provided here for students of Class 10 to have quick revision for the chapter “Pairs of Linear Equations in Two Variables”. This chapter will help you to determine the values of the unknown variables with the help of various methods, such as the Graphical method and Algebraic methods. An example of pairs of linear equations will be: Rohan bought 2 pencils and 1 pen for Rs.10, whereas Sam bought 4 pencils and 2 pens for Rs. 20, so what is the price of each pen and pencil? Now to find the solution to this question, we can take pen and pencil as two different variables and write the linear equations for each condition. Hence, by solving the pair of linear equations in two variables, we will get the price of a pen and pencil. Let us go through the article to understand how to solve such a pair of equations.
Basics Revisited
Equation
An equation is a statement that two mathematical expressions having one or more variables are equal.
Linear Equation
Equations in which the powers of all the variables involved…
**Crawled sources (internet):**
- [Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables Class 10: Chapter 3](https://byjus.com/cbse-notes/cbse-class-10-maths-notes-chapter-3-pair-of-linear-equations-in-two-variables/)
- [NCERT Solutions for Class 10 Maths Chapter 3 Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables](https://byjus.com/ncert-solutions-class-10-maths/chapter-3-linear-equations-in-two-variables/)
- [Substitution & Elimination Methods for Solving Linear Equations - CBSE Class 10](https://deekshalearning.com/maths/algebraic-methods-of-solving-a-pair-of-linear-equations/)

sections.title: 2. Key Definitions
sections.body:
**Definitions extracted from crawled pages:**

- An equation is a statement that two mathematical expressions having one or more variables are equal.
- Equations in which the powers of all the variables involved are one are called linear equations. The degree of a linear equation is always one.
- The general form of a linear equation in two variables is ax + by + c = 0, where a and b cannot be zero simultaneously.
- Example: The cost of 5 pens and 7 pencils is Rs.50 whereas the cost of 7 pens and 5 pencils is Rs. 65. Represent the given word problem in linear equations form.
- Solution: Let us say the cost of 1 pen is Rs.x, and the cost of 1 pencil is Rs.y
- Hence, 5x + 7y = 50 and 7x + 5y = 65 are the pairs of linear equations in two variables as per the given word problem.
- The solution of a linear equation in two variables is a pair of values (x, y), one for ‘x’ and the other for ‘y’, which makes the two sides of the equation equal.
- Eg: If 2x + y = 4, then (0,4) is one of its solutions as it satisfies the equation. A linear equation in two variables has infinitely many solutions.

sections.title: 4. Formulas / Theorems
sections.body:
**Formulas / relations found:**

- The general form of a linear equation in two variables is ax + by + c = 0, where a and b cannot be zero simultaneously.
- 5x + 7y = 50
- 7x + 5y = 65
- Hence, 5x + 7y = 50 and 7x + 5y = 65 are the pairs of linear equations in two variables as per the given word pro
- Eg: If 2x + y = 4, then (0,4) is one of its solutions as it satisfies the equation. A linear eq
- 2x – y + 1 = 0
- ⇒ y = 2x + 1
- Step 1: Put the value of x = 0, such that y = 1.

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables**._

**Practice questions from crawled sources:**

1. (i) express one variable in terms of the other using one of the equations. In this case, y = 2x + 1.
2. (ii) substitute for this variable (y) in the second equation to get a linear equation in one variable, x. x + 2 × (2x + 1) = 12
3. (iii) Solve the linear equation in one variable to find the value of that variable.
4. (iv) Substitute this value in one of the equations to get the value of the other variable.
5. 1. Solve age-related problems 2. Calculate speed 3. Geometry-related problems 4. Work, time and wage-related problems 5. Calculate money, percentage-related sums
6. 1. Aftab tells his daughter, “Seven years ago, I was seven times as old as you were then. Also, three years from now, I shall be three times as old as you will be.” (Isn’t this interesting?) Represent this situation algebraically and graphically.
7. 2. The coach of a cricket team buys 3 bats and 6 balls for Rs.3900. Later, she buys another bat and 3 more balls of the same kind for Rs.1300. Represent this situation algebraically and geometrically.
8. 3. The cost of 2 kg of apples and 1kg of grapes on a day was found to be Rs.160. After a month, the cost of 4 kg of apples and 2 kg of grapes is Rs.300. Represent the situation algebraically and geometrically.
9. 1. Form the pair of linear equations in the following problems, and find their solutions graphically.
10. (i) 10 students of Class X took part in a Mathematics quiz. If the number of girls is 4 more than the number of boys, find the number of boys and girls who took part in the quiz.

**Additional syllabus-based set** _(auto-generated for review — Phase-II ingest)_

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Graphical method** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Graphical method** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Substitution method** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables.
